expected_properties: When Secs reaches 59, the next clock edge sets Secs to 0 and increments Mins by 1.
If Mins is also 59 when Secs wraps, Hours increments by 1 while Secs and Mins reset to 0.
Hours does not change when only Secs wraps and Mins < 59.
additional_cases.goal: Validate correct boundary behavior for seconds, minutes, and hour roll‑over without assuming exact cycle counts.
notes: Uses observed values of Hours, Mins, Secs to detect the transition.
Conservative observation: wait until Secs==59 is seen, then capture the next state.
preconditions: Reset has been released and the design is in a stable post‑reset state.
stimulus_intent: Drive the clock until Secs reports 59.
Observe the next clock edge to verify wrap‑around and minute increment.

stimulus_program.text: Allow any post‑reset transients to settle.
Capture Hours, Mins, Secs when Secs==59.
Advance one clock edge to see wrap‑around.

timing_assumptions: Do not assume a fixed number of cycles to reach 59; rely on observed Secs value.

expected_properties: Observed outputs or protocol-visible signals settle to a stable, non-X post‑reset state.
No fixed‑cycle completion is assumed during reset recovery.
baseline_case_enrichments.goal: Establish a stable post‑reset baseline before functional checking.
notes: Reset polarity may still be heuristic if the contract marks it ambiguous.
stimulus_intent: Assert the detected reset using the inferred polarity.
Release reset conservatively and observe interface stabilization.

stimulus_program.text: Assert reset for two clock cycles.
Observe a few clock edges for output stabilization.
Capture Hours, Mins, Secs after reset.

timing_assumptions: Do not assume completion before it becomes externally visible.

expected_properties: Externally visible outputs remain defined and can change across conservative clocked observation windows.
The plan observes progress without assuming a fixed‑cycle protocol or hidden internal state access.
baseline_case_enrichments.goal: Observe clock‑driven state progress after reset or conservative initialization.
notes: Case intent is conservative when the contract is weak or timing is unresolved.
Case relies on deterministic clock‑driven observation because no non‑control inputs were resolved.
stimulus_intent: Release reset if present and observe a small number of conservative clock edges for externally visible progress.

stimulus_program.text: Observe five clock cycles to see any change in Hours, Mins, Secs.
Capture output values after observation window.

timing_assumptions: Do not assume completion before it becomes externally visible.

planning_notes: Added edge case to verify wrap‑around behavior at 59 seconds/minutes.
Enriched reset and basic cases with explicit stimulus programs and additional notes.
